validation_rules: Every required route must have a runnable command and at least one public docs reference.
Every required route must resolve to at least one receipt reference.
The first-run sequence must be present and ordinal sorted.
Release authority, provider-call authority, private-data equivalence, private source bodies, trading advice, and whole-system correctness claims are rejected.

doctrine_population_status.authority_boundary: Structured doctrine population binds source-backed capsule, mechanism, and atlas source rows; generated atlas docs remain builder-owned projections.

public_private_boundary.public_inputs: synthetic route cards, route receipt refs, route policy, and public docs refs

standard_payload.ontology: A public cold-reader route-map organ that validates Microcosm first-run route order, command refs, docs refs, receipt refs, public runtime refs, negative cases, and authority ceilings without private macro context.
standard_payload.teleology: Make the first ten minutes runnable and evidence-backed for a cold technical reader while keeping route maps projection-only and source-open without secrets, provider payloads, account/session material, browser/HUD live access, or release authority.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: A glossy onboarding route list, status-card shortcut, or receipt archive that lacks runnable commands, receipt-backed route ids, first-run ordering, negative cases, public/private boundaries, or real runtime validation.
failure_modes: route card admitted without a runnable command ref
route card admitted without public docs refs
required route id missing from the first-run route map
first-run sequence missing compile_project or no longer ordinal sorted
route id accepted without at least one evidence receipt ref
route receipt row exists but its receipt_refs are empty or disconnected from the route id
cold-reader route map accepted as route-registry authority
release, publication, provider-call, private-data equivalence, trading or financial advice, or whole-system correctness overclaim accepted
private macro source body, raw seed body, provider payload body, secret value, account/session state, browser/HUD live access, or credential-equivalent material exported through the route map
secret-exclusion scan omitted or body material embedded in route-map receipts
synthetic receipt stand-in accepted instead of real runtime receipt refs
exported route-map bundle accepted without route_map, route_receipts, route_policy, manifest, public runtime refs, and runtime validation receipt
cold-reader route map claims product progress from route-list metadata without validating the public first-run command chain
receipt duplicates route or macro body text instead of carrying public refs, verdicts, hashes, and omission boundaries

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; existing authority_ceiling remains the operative claim ceiling.

anti_claim: Cold-reader route maps validate public first-run route projection only; they do not become route registry authority, expose private sources, authorize release, call providers, mutate source projects, or prove whole-system correctness.

author.purpose: create a valid Cold-reader route map instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Cold-reader route map without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_cold_reader_route_map and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections

source_authority_ref: microcosm-substrate/AXIOMS.md#1 (JSON is contract; Markdown is projection)

doctrine_kind_envelope.note: This standard conforms to the universal doctrine-kind envelope; markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are generated projections of this JSON source.

anti_claims: Cold-reader route maps validate public first-run route projection only; they do not become route registry authority, expose private sources, authorize release, call providers, mutate source projects, or prove whole-system correctness.

Active v2 standard status is a source JSON contract state only; it does not expand release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, product-readiness, or whole-system correctness authority beyond the authority_ceiling.

runtime_acceptance_refs.uplift_basis: registry-backed source standard already carried accepted, active, source-body-import, or body-floor status; this update narrows it into the active v2 JSON contract shape without raising its authority ceiling.
